vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current physical state. P1 (Yellow Pot) and P2/P4 (Black Pot) contain no visible botanical specimens. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but the Pothos is absent or non-viable.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos absent; only rabbit anchor remains)

plant_audit.P1: No plant detected. Soil surface appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2: No plant detected. Soil surface appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present. No Pothos foliage visible.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears dry and granular across all visible pots.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments observed in the central black pot (P3/P4 area).

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: Initial state shows a single green stem/leaf in the black pot. This specimen undergoes rapid chlorosis and collapse.
temporal_deltas.t3_to_current: The specimen has completely vanished or withered into the substrate. The biome is now effectively dormant/barren.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome has suffered a total collapse of all registered specimens. The presence of eggshell fragments suggests a failed attempt at soil amendment or a non-botanical intervention that did not prevent the systemic loss of the plants.
anomalies: Presence of eggshell fragments in P3/P4.
Total absence of registered plant life.
vision_report.narrative_description: As the observer, I first performed a baseline scan of the pots to identify any remaining organic matter. Upon validation, it is clear that the botanical registry is no longer reflected in the physical environment. The sequence documents a rapid decline from a single, struggling specimen to a completely barren state. The environment is currently devoid of living plant matter, and the 'white rabbit' anchor is the only remaining identifier of the original biome setup.
